title: The demo-up frontend tier — /demo-up now brings up the full UI: next-web-app + studio-desk per-demo (a CACHED Docker image from the UNMODIFIED platform Dockerfile, offset ports, minted-pk + offset-URL baked) + ant-academy natively (Clerk-free via BENCHMARK_VISUAL_BYPASS), with a 12 GB Docker-VM pre-flight, a --no-ui escape, and the frontend ports registered in M18's verify net — all ZERO platform-repo edits. Owns the frontend-emitting gen_injected_override.py, the per-demo serial-before-up build in up-injected.sh, the tooling-owned transient .dockerignore, the native-academy launcher, and the net-new corpus/ops/demo/frontend-tier.md.
position_note: M19 is the 4th of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' (M16->M20) and the largest. It completes the demo family (up -> snapshot -> seed -> SEE-IT-IN-A-BROWSER -> down). Depends on M18 (so the verify net covers the new frontend services). Parallel-with: none. M20 (lifecycle convergence / cold-start) is the next + final milestone.
kind: a tooling milestone (the frontend-emitting override + the per-demo frontend build orchestration + the native-academy launcher + the verify-net frontend rows) + one net-new corpus doc + the demo-up SKILL update. ALL testable logic lives in the SEPARATE nested .agentspace/rosetta-extensions repo (python: stack-injection/gen_injected_override.py; bash: demo-stack/up-injected.sh build_frontends/tag-guard/transient-.dockerignore/12GB-preflight/DEMO_NO_UI, demo-stack/ant-academy.sh native-launch+fallback, stack-verify/lib/services.sh frontend rows, demo-stack/rosetta-demo academy-stop-on-down; tooling-owned demo-stack/frontend/next-web.dockerignore). The rosetta m19/frontend-tier branch carries only corpus/ops/demo/frontend-tier.md (net-new) + the demo-up SKILL.md update + the index rows (ops README + CLAUDE.md) + the milestone tracking docs.

tests.language: python (stdlib unittest, driving the bash via subprocess + a docker/npm/node stub) + shellcheck/py_compile on the 4 touched bash scripts + markdown (the net-new frontend-tier.md = the rosetta deliverable). No Go touched.
tests.go_note: Unchanged by M19 — M19 touched only Python + bash + docs. All 4 Go modules still build + pass -count=1; gofmt + go vet clean (regression-checked at close, not re-counted).
tests.python_note: +65 collected — the M19 UI-tier coverage: demo-stack/tests/test_frontend_build.py (the per-demo build behaviour, the tag-guard reuse, the transient .dockerignore lifecycle, the DEMO_NO_UI escape, and TestZeroPlatformRepoEdit — a real-git-repo git-status-clean guard on BOTH the success and the failed/aborted RETURN-scoped-trap path + a git check-ignore fence on the pk overlay), demo-stack/tests/test_ant_academy.py (the native launch, the documented fallback for absent-clone/no-deps/old-node, the stdio-detach pipe-hang regression, the no-Clerk-keys overlay edge), stack-injection/tests/test_injection.py TestFrontendTier (the frontend_lines/build_lines emit, offset ports, demo-N images, with_ui/--no-ui, the removed stale REUSE_DEV entry), stack-verify/tests/test_verify.py TestFrontendTierRegistration (the next-web/studio-desk rows + the offset matrix + the scope-filter-out on --no-ui). gen_injected_override.py (the one pure-Python touched file) measured 98% (the single miss is the __main__ guard — saturated). The 4 M19-touched suites = 193 passed. Trajectory: build 186 -> harden 193 (+7) -> close 193 (+0, doc-only close fixes).
tests.shellcheck: all 4 M19-touched bash scripts shellcheck-CLEAN (-S warning): demo-stack/up-injected.sh, demo-stack/ant-academy.sh, demo-stack/rosetta-demo, stack-verify/lib/services.sh.
tests.py_compile: stack-injection/gen_injected_override.py + the 4 M19 test files py_compile CLEAN.
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean, sequential, on the 4 milestone-touched suites (193 tests). Deterministic — 193 passed on every run. 0 flakes. (pytest-randomly not installed in this repo; the gate is the sequential-repeat form, which catches shared temp dirs / ports / caches.)

coverage.note: Bash tooling is path-enumerated via the Python harness (subprocess + a docker/npm/node stub; no kcov — matches M16/M17/M18 bash-tooling practice, coverage as candidate-finder not a numeric gate). The one pure-Python file (gen_injected_override.py) is measured: 98% (only the __main__ guard). Harden ran a single pass (recorded in progress.md); the loop stopped after the full Step-2b scan found no further seam worth pinning.
coverage.seams_pinned: the frontend override emit (frontend_lines/build_lines, offset ports, demo-N images, with_ui/--no-ui, the removed stale REUSE_DEV entry), the per-demo build behaviour (offset URLs + minted pk assembled, tag-guard skip-when-present / build-when-absent for BOTH frontends), the ZERO-PLATFORM-EDIT invariant (real-git-repo git-status-clean on success AND on a failed/aborted build via the RETURN-scoped trap, plus a git check-ignore fence the pk overlay can never be tracked), the native-academy launch + the documented fallback (absent-clone / no-deps / old-node / no-Clerk-keys), the stdio-detach pipe-hang regression, the 12 GB preflight non-fatal warn, and the verify-net frontend-row registration + the --no-ui scope-out.

zero_platform_edit_invariant_verificatio.verdict: GREEN — the tooling writes NOTHING tracked into next-web-app / studio-desk / ant-academy / platform / any Go-service repo. The repos are used as a Docker build CONTEXT only, with UNMODIFIED Dockerfiles.
zero_platform_edit_invariant_verificatio.next_web_pk: next-web's Dockerfile.dev declares ARGs only for the 3 URLs (no pk ARG — verified) -> the minted pk rides a GITIGNORED apps/web/.env.local overlay (matched by apps/web/.gitignore's .env* rule), read by `next build`, removed by a RETURN-scoped trap. Pinned by TestZeroPlatformRepoEdit (git status stays empty) + a git check-ignore fence.
zero_platform_edit_invariant_verificatio.studio_desk_pk: studio-desk's Dockerfile.dev DOES declare VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY as an ARG -> pk passed straight as a build-arg; no overlay, no .dockerignore touch (studio-desk already ships one excluding node_modules/dist/.git).
zero_platform_edit_invariant_verificatio.transient_dockerignore: next-web ships no .dockerignore (2.8 GB context); a TOOLING-OWNED one (rosetta-extensions/demo-stack/frontend/next-web.dockerignore) is copied in TRANSIENTLY with a non-clobber guard (refuses to overwrite a pre-existing repo .dockerignore) and removed by the RETURN-scoped trap — so the repo stays byte-clean even on a FAILED/ABORTED build. Pinned by test_next_web_failed_build_still_removes_pk_env_local_and_dockerignore + the no-.dockerignore and pre-existing-.dockerignore cases in TestZeroPlatformRepoEdit.
zero_platform_edit_invariant_verificatio.ant_academy: the per-demo env is a GITIGNORED code/.env.local overlay (code/.gitignore ignores .env.local); zero academy-repo edits. The bypass (BENCHMARK_VISUAL_BYPASS) is the repo's own dev-only flag, set in the environment, not a file edit.
zero_platform_edit_invariant_verificatio.unmodified_dockerfiles: both builds use `docker build -f $ctx/Dockerfile.dev ... $ctx` against the platform clone's UNMODIFIED Dockerfile; no perl/sed/edit of any tracked platform file. (Contrast the injected Go services, which DO patch a clone's Dockerfile — but those are clones under the demo stack dir, not the dev platform repos; the frontends touch nothing.)
zero_platform_edit_invariant_verificatio.out_of_scope_true_zero_rebuild: the 'true zero-rebuild' (one image, port/pk switched at RUNTIME) would require platform-source changes (runtime rewrites in next.config.mjs, an absolute SSR origin, a window.__ENV shim + explicit publishableKey on <ClerkProvider>, output:standalone) — REAL platform edits with PR/review/prod risk, FORBIDDEN for the demo tooling. Documented in frontend-tier.md as a user-owned optional upstream PR (the v1.4 deploy-CI precedent), NOT built in M19. The honest residual (one ~3-min cached build per new demo-N) is the accepted cost of staying tooling-only.

delivered.frontend_tier_md: corpus/ops/demo/frontend-tier.md (net-new, ~140 lines, dual-level + code-cited): the UI-tier table (next-web :3000+ / studio-desk :9100+:9000+ / ant-academy :3077+, how each runs + its Clerk-free auth), the per-demo-build rationale + the honest cached-build residual, the 12 GB Docker-VM prereq + the non-fatal preflight (#M19-D5), the pk/URL baking table (next-web .env.local vs studio-desk build-arg, #M19-D3; the transient non-clobber .dockerignore, #M19-D4) + the RETURN-scoped-cleanup callout (the failed-build path too), the native ant-academy section + documented fallback (#M19-D6), the verify-net coverage (scoped iff UI on, #M19-D7), where the tooling lives, and the out-of-scope true-zero-rebuild PR. Indexed in ops README + root CLAUDE.md; cross-linked from the demo README + the demo-up SKILL; all 6 outbound .md links resolve.
delivered.gen_injected_override: stack-injection/gen_injected_override.py — appends the UI tier (next-web-app + studio-desk) to the injected override as `profiles: !override [graphql]` services with per-demo built images (image: demo-N-*, build: !reset null, pull_policy: never, mem_limit: 1g, offset ports: !override); --no-ui clears the tier; removed the stale next-web-app REUSE_DEV entry (#M19-D2). The frontends are APPENDED (not walked out of cfg) because they live in the frontend/studio-desk/all profiles, not the demo's graphql profile (#M19-D1).
delivered.up_injected_build: demo-stack/up-injected.sh — build_frontends() builds the two frontends SERIALLY + BEFORE compose up (out of the parallel Go fan-out so the build RAM spike never overlaps), from the unmodified Dockerfiles with offset-URL build-args + the minted pk, TAG-GUARDED for cache reuse (a re-up costs seconds; only a new demo-N pays the ~3-min build), NON-FATAL (a UI build failure warns but never aborts the backend bring-up). Plus the 12 GB VM preflight (non-fatal, DEMO_VM_MIN_GIB override) + the DEMO_NO_UI escape (threaded to gen_injected_override --no-ui so override + build always agree). HERE resolves via BASH_SOURCE + a UP_INJECTED_LIB_ONLY test seam (#M19-D8).
delivered.ant_academy: demo-stack/ant-academy.sh — launches ant-academy NATIVELY on 3077+offset Clerk-free (BENCHMARK_VISUAL_BYPASS + REQUIRE_ORGANIZATION_MEMBERSHIP=0), default-on + non-fatal + degrades to a documented manual step (absent-clone / no-deps / old-node), with a gitignored code/.env.local overlay. The launch detaches ALL THREE stdio fds on the subshell to release the caller's pipe (the background-job pipe-hang fix, #M19-D9). Wired into up-injected.sh (start) + rosetta-demo down (stop — it's a process, not a container).
delivered.next_web_dockerignore: demo-stack/frontend/next-web.dockerignore — the tooling-owned context trim (2.8 GB -> <100 MB) applied transiently to next-web.
delivered.verify_net_rows: stack-verify/lib/services.sh — the next-web-app (:3000) + studio-desk (:9100) rows, offset + project-rewritten like every other service, scoped out by STACK_SERVICES on a --no-ui demo (#M19-D7). Fills the hook M18 explicitly left for M19.
delivered.skill_update: .claude/skills/demo-up/SKILL.md — UI tier now in scope (description + argument-hint + Mission), the 12 GB prereq + --no-ui guidance, the frontend ports in the verify step.

extensions.module: rosetta-extensions — all M19 testable logic (the frontend-emitting override + the per-demo frontend build orchestration + the native-academy launcher + the verify-net frontend rows + the tooling-owned .dockerignore + the M19 test suites).

A clean scope boundary, not a deferral. 0 orphan TODO/FIXME in either repo.
review.code_quality: GREEN — gen_injected_override.py / up-injected.sh / ant-academy.sh / services.sh consistent with M16/M17/M18 conventions (BASH_SOURCE resolution, stack-dev + legacy fallback, non-fatal-helper pattern, documented manual fallback). The zero-platform-edit invariant is structurally enforced (gitignored .env.local, transient trap-cleaned .dockerignore with non-clobber guard, unmodified Dockerfiles, pk-as-build-arg only where the Dockerfile declares it). py_compile OK; shellcheck CLEAN (4 scripts). 0 must-fix. (The probe_service 5xx-regex nuance in services.sh is pre-existing c95bce4, not M19-touched.)

review.decision_triage: M19-D3 (pk-path split), D4 (transient non-clobber .dockerignore), D5 (non-fatal 12 GB preflight), D6 (native academy + bypass + fallback), D7 (verify scoped iff UI on) blended into frontend-tier.md with (#M19-DK) back-ref tags. review.adversarial: Phase 2c — the harden pass already pinned the load-bearing invariant adversarially (TestZeroPlatformRepoEdit: real-git-repo git-status-clean on the success AND failed/aborted-build paths + a git check-ignore fence). The close re-examined 3 further scenarios (the RETURN trap clobbering/leaking across the two serial builds; a stale academy pidfile making a re-up think it's running; platform/.env absent making an invalid overlay) — ALL already handled in the shipped code (trap cleared per-return; dead-pid gates via kill -0; Clerk keys optional under BYPASS). No new finding required code changes. Scenarios recorded in decisions.md.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — knowledge/plan/releases/01.3b-dress-rehearsal/m19-frontend-tier/audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-09-m19-close.md. The only open item is the inherited cross-release DEF-M10-01 (S3 blobs + cloud store -> v1.4, signed) — untouched by M19 (M19 touched the frontend bring-up surface, NOT the snapshot-store/S3 area), all 4 aging triggers negative, authority intact. 0 repeat, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out.

completeness_ledger.done_fate1: all 8 deliverables: gen_injected_override emits next-web + studio-desk, up-injected builds the two frontends (serial-before-up, offset URLs + minted pk, tag-guarded cache), the sibling transient .dockerignore, ant-academy native launch + documented fallback, the 12 GB VM preflight, the frontend ports registered in the verify net, the --no-ui escape, and the net-new corpus/ops/demo/frontend-tier.md + the demo-up SKILL update. Plus the harden zero-platform-edit deepening (+7 tests) + the close Fate-1 (the 5 decision ref-tags + the adversarial-scenario records).
completeness_ledger.confirmed_covered_fate2: none — M19 had no Fate-2 routing (M18 had already routed the frontend ports TO M19; M19 landed them).
completeness_ledger.escape_hatch_release_scope_breaking: none introduced by M19. The true-zero-rebuild upstream platform PR is a documented OUT-of-scope boundary (editing platform repos is forbidden by the release invariant), NOT a deferral. DEF-M10-01 (cloud snapshot store + S3 media blob bytes) is INHERITED from v1.2, signed -> v1.4, orthogonal to M19 (M19 touched the frontend bring-up surface, NOT the snapshot-store/S3 area), not aged out. Unchanged.

notes: M19 is the 4th + largest milestone of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' — it completes the demo family by making a demo actually DEMOABLE: /demo-up now brings up the full UI tier (next-web + studio-desk per-demo + ant-academy native), all Clerk-free, on offset ports, on a 16 GB Mac. The defining constraint was the LOAD-BEARING ZERO-PLATFORM-EDIT INVARIANT: the tooling writes nothing tracked into any platform repo — the minted pk rides a gitignored apps/web/.env.local, the .dockerignore is transient + non-clobber + RETURN-scoped-trap-removed (so even a FAILED build leaves the repo byte-clean), the platform repos are a build CONTEXT only with UNMODIFIED Dockerfiles. The harden pass pinned this with TestZeroPlatformRepoEdit (a real-git-repo git-status-clean guard on both the success and the failed-build path + a git check-ignore fence), mutation-verified non-no-op. The close confirmed the invariant holds + meaningful, re-examined 3 further adversarial scenarios (all already handled), and was otherwise the cleanest possible shape: 0 scope / 0 code / 0 docs / 0 tests findings, 6 decision-triage (5 ref-tags + 4 archives), doc-only. The honest residual (one ~3-min cached build per new demo-N) is the accepted cost of staying tooling-only; the true-zero-rebuild upstream PR is documented as a user-owned OUT boundary. Reconciled the dress-rehearsal-m19 tag from the build HEAD 32b1ae8 to the final HEAD (the tag trailed the harden commit 4f96ddd), re-consuming stack-demo to it. Deferral re-audit GREEN. Go 736 unchanged, Python 273->338 collected (+65, the M19 UI-tier suites), flake 0 (5/5). Next: M20 (lifecycle convergence — the next + FINAL milestone of v1.3b).
